finding_results.notes: The current chapter had already resolved both cited echoes prior to this pass. Snapshot line 17's verbatim calibration echo ("Sure") was replaced with "Yeah," Tammy said. "I do." — a loaded brush-off that preserves the same flat, suspicious function without reproducing the style-bible example line. Snapshot line 25's near-echo ("Something was off") was replaced entirely by pattern-recognition narration beginning at line 49 ("It had stopped feeling random"), which conveys cautious suspicion through Tammy's observational cataloguing voice rather than the calibration phrasing. No other Tammy calibration lines appear verbatim or near-verbatim anywhere in the chapter. Additionally, three contraction-drift fixes were applied within the dialogue-pass scope: Lola's "You would think" corrected to "You'd think" (line 27) per her HIGH contraction level; narration restored to "Carmen didn't look up" (line 21) and "she'd used" (line 47) to match the snapshot's colloquial close-third texture.

summary: Single finding (style-bible echo) was already resolved in the current chapter text — Tammy's flat brush-off and suspicion beat use original phrasing that matches her voice without reproducing calibration-only example lines. Three additional contraction-drift fixes applied: one in Lola's dialogue (HIGH contraction level enforced) and two in close-third narration where the chapter had drifted formal from the snapshot's established texture. Heading contract preserved. No new continuity issues introduced.
